response_0.content: What is the reasoning behind not letting people who have been to jail vote?

In most democracies, people who have been to jail are not allowed to vote, because they have been penalized for a crime and are therefore seen as being unqualified to participate in the democratic process.  Some people argue that preventing these individuals from voting violates their rights, while others believe that it is necessary to prevent former prisoners from unfairly influencing elections.  Either way, preventing prisoners from voting is a longstanding policy in many countries.

response_1.content: What is the reasoning behind not letting people who have been to jail vote?

There are two main reasons: One is that voting makes people more invested in the status quo. They would not want to destroy the status quo, and in some cases their status quo might not be very good. But there’s also another reason: people who have served time in jail tend to be more marginalized in society. It can be difficult for them to find a job, and the average income is much lower, and those are factors that make it harder for them to vote.

I’ll describe the basic version:  You start with a set of six dice, each of which has the numbers 1 through 6. From this set of numbers, you select five to place on your play sheet, which goes on the side of the table. Since this is Yahtzee, you also need to select a 6-point number to place on top of the play sheet.  You do this in order to win the game, and you win by rolling 5 or 6 dice with the matching number, or by rolling the same numbers as the dice placed in the center of the table.  The order that you roll your dice can also matter, but I’ll let you read about that on your own if you’re interested.  If the game ends in a tie, each side gets half of the points.

response_0.content: What are Newton's three laws of motion?

Newton's laws of motion describe the relationship between forces acting on objects and the resulting motion of objects. Newton's three laws of motion are: 
I.	A body at rest remains at rest and a body in motion continues in motion in a straight line at a constant speed, unless acted upon by a net external force. 
II.	The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the magnitude (size) of the object's net force, is indirectly proportional to the object's mass, and inversely proportional to the coefficient of friction. 
III.	For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.
Can you summarize his first law of motion in a way a fourth grader would understand?

The first law of motion states that if an object is not being acted upon by a net force, then its velocity (speed and direction of movement) will remain the same.  For example, if you hold your car keys stationary in the air, they will not move, because they are in a state of free fall since the ground is exerting the force of gravity on them, which just acts to maintain a constant velocity. This is known as "inertia."
Fourth graders don't have car keys. Can you use an example that is relevant to children?

An example that would be relevant to fourth graders is that A soccer ball will roll across a flat surface at a constant speed, if it is not affected by any net force. This is because the surface of the earth exerts a natural force of gravity on the soccer ball, causing it to roll downwards which allows it to maintain a constant speed.
Using the soccer ball example, can you explain the second law of motion?

Newton's second law of motion describes the relationship between force acting on an object and the resulting acceleration (change in velocity) of the object. A large force will produce a greater acceleration of the object.

The formula for the second law of motion is F=ma, where F is the force acting on an object, m is the mass of the object, and a is the resulting acceleration.  

The second law of motion can be illustrated with the example of a soccer ball, as follows:

When a soccer ball is lying still on the ground, it is not being acted upon by any net force, and thus does not accelerate.  If at this moment you place your hand beneath the ball and apply a force upwards, they ball will begin to accelerate upwards, since a net force is now acting upon it, and the combination of its mass and this force result in acceleration upwards.

response_0.content: What are some advantages of contact lenses over eyeglasses?

There are many advantages of contact lenses over eyeglasses, including the fact that contact lenses are practically invisible, are more comfortable to wear, and do not impede or obscure vision as much as eyeglasses, as well as giving a more natural appearance. Additionally, contact lenses can easily be cleaned and simply removed for cleaning or replacement, while glasses cannot be easily cleaned or replaced in this manner. Contact lenses are also much more affordable than eyeglasses, and provide significantly better vision correction than traditional eyeglasses. Several types of vision correction are also possible with contact lenses, which is not possible with traditional eyeglasses. Finally, contact lenses have minimal adverse impact on the eyes, and pose significantly less risk of injury than eyeglasses. Contact lenses are the most convenient and optimal form of vision correction for many people.
How do eyeglasses impede/obscure vision?

There are several ways in which traditional eyeglasses can impede or obscure vision.  Eyeglasses are typically thicker and heavier than contact lenses, resulting in more obstruction of peripheral vision and more trouble tilting the head for side-to-side vision.  Additionally, traditional eyeglasses usually have large and thick lenses which reduce visibility of objects and people located directly in front of the wearer, as well as obstructing the wearer's own view of their face.  Additionally, modern eyeglass frames are often made of dark or reflective materials which further interfere with vision and detract from the aesthetic appearance of the wearer.  Generally speaking, eyeglasses can significantly impair or obstruct both central and peripheral vision for the wearer, and are significantly less convenient and practical than contact lenses in many ways.

response_0.content: Can you explain what a comet is?

Oh, sure!  A comet is a small icy rock that orbits the Sun, and on occasion it passes by Earth.  And because the Sun’s gravity is much stronger, it curves the comet’s orbit.  When it’s time for the comet to make its closest approach to the Sun, it gets very close to the Sun, and this causes a very cold spot to appear on the comet’s surface.  Because ice can only stay solid at very low temperatures, a chunk of ice breaks away from the comet, and this chunk of ice is what we see when the comet makes its closest approach to the Sun and then starts to move away from the Sun.  Now that chunk of ice is called a “coma”.  And because it was first seen only when it was near the Sun, it was called a “comet”, and this is how comets got their name!  They are usually very small, but they can get quite bright, and they can also appear to change their color and even explode.
Do they make their own light?
No, they don’t have their own light source.  They only glow because the sunlight is reflecting off the surface.  As they get farther away from the Sun, their light gets fainter.
So they reflect light like the moon?

Actually, comets glow because the ice turns into gas.  Think about the way ice cubes get very warm if you put them near a fire, and they then start to drip water.  That’s very similar to what happens with a comet, and this is why it sometimes gets bright, but it never glows at night!  But while we can see the glowing from the comet near the Sun, the only thing that we can see at night is the tail.  Because the gas in the comet can’t all escape at once, as it’s moving away from the Sun, it leaves a long trail of gas and dust.  So we see the tail of the comet, not the comet itself.  It’s really more like it gets obscured by a really long piece of gas.

Bauhaus was an architectural style that was popular in Europe between 1919 and 1933. It was founded by a German architect named Walter Gropius, who designed several buildings in a very simple style. It became associated with the art movements of Dadaism, Constructivism, and De Stijl. Why do you want to know about this?
Are there any good examples still in use today?

This is an interesting question. Bauhaus is probably most widely recognized today in the form of buildings that are called "International Style" and "High Modern". These styles were built after Bauhaus. I have to say though that it's a tricky question whether these styles are still in use today. In architectural theory, I could see how there's still some relevance to the idea of simplicity and clean lines. But in most cases, these buildings that could be called International Style or High Modern were popular up until the mid-1980s or so. After that, people started to feel that these buildings, while exciting at the time, started to look a bit tired. For that reason, people moved away from that style and now embrace a new one that is a kind of exaggeration of the International Style, called Deconstructivism.

response_0.content: Can you explain the origins of the USSR in depth, please?

Sure, the USSR (also known as the Soviet Union), or Union of Soviet Socialist Republics was a nation in Europe and Asia from 1922 until 1991 when it was dissolved.  It was a communist political state founded on socialist principles by the Russian revolutionary Vladimir Lenin and centered around the major cities of Moscow and Saint Petersburg.  It was a geographically enormous country comprised of around 15 republics containing over 100 million citizens in its time, and was a major world power during much of the 20th century.  It played a pivotal role in the World Wars I and II and expanded its sphere of influence across Eastern Europe in the era of the Cold War.  

The USSR was formed following the 1917 Russian revolution, when Russia was ruled by the democratic, modern and socialist Bolshevik party led by Lenin.  In 1919, the civil war following the revolution made the Russian state unstable and vulnerable.  This political crisis precipitated the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk which ended Russian involvement in the First World War.  It was under this unstable and turbulent climate that the fledgling communist government decided to adopt the additional form of a state union, a solution that would stabilize and consolidate the region.  This new union began with the Russian SFSR, which became the Soviet Union on December 30, 1922.  The relations between the Ukraine (founded in 1917), Belorussia (1920), Georgia (1921), and Armenia and other central Asian republics all gradually joined the union by 1922, however, these nations had varying degrees of sovereignty and autonomy within the union.  The final additions took place in late 1940, when Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania were forcibly incorporated into the USSR from annexation by Nazi Germany during World War II.  

An enormous and competing ideological and political spectrum kept the USSR fractured over its 79-year duration.  From its very origins, the USSR was made up of a diversity of cultures and nationalities, and this ideological and cultural division made the state a continual battleground between different forces and factions.  It nearly imploded during the 1920s and 30s with the ascension of Josef Stalin, who created a brutal political regime designed to suppress dissent.  The USSR remained an extremely authoritarian state throughout most of its existence, a stark contrast to democratic capitalist western nations.  However, the severity of the regime diminished during the postwar decades, coming to a head with the fall of the Berlin Wall and the subsequent dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.  This collapse was precipitated by the rapid and widespread growth of capitalism that gradually undercut the economic viability and social structures of the Soviet power.  Since then, Russia has engaged in an extended process of democratic, social, and economic reforms, and has adopted market economics, while Georgia, Azerbaijan, and other Central Asian countries have charted new paths to global influence and improvement in democratic governance.  

The Soviet Union was formed over one of the most turbulent and significant eras of 20th century world history, and was a prominent and influential force for political, economic, and military power across Europe and much of the rest of the world.  While it best represents an era of Cold War tension and global escalation of communism, the USSR was also the culmination of a centuries-long conflict over the role of Russia in global affairs, and its dissolution continues to shape geostrategic developments into the 21st century.  The legacy of the Soviet Union is a complicated and complex one, involving the role of democracy, economic disparity, collective identity, and authoritarianism, reflecting some of the fundamental internal conflicts facing the global human community.

I hope this provides some helpful background on the USSR.  Please let me know if I can clarify or elaborate in any way.

response_1.content: Can you explain the origins of the USSR in depth, please?

The USSR, or Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, was founded in 1922 under the leadership of Vladimir Lenin, out of the Russian Revolution and the overthrow of the Romanov Dynasty.  The goal of the USSR was to establish a modern communist state in which the means of production are collectively owned and operated, with an egalitarian system and full social empowerment for all the people.  The founding republics included the Russian Federation, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Estonia, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Latvia, Lithuania, Moldova, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Ukraine and Uzbekistan.  

Over the next four decades Stalin exerted an iron grip on the USSR, implementing a combination of economic collectivism, de-Stalinization and anti-religious campaigns, while imposing a totalitarian political system and developing state-sponsored forms of repression and violence.  In 1991 the Soviet Union was dissolved, leading to disaster and economic meltdown.  The USSR’s main successor states remain today as the Russian Federation and 14 other independent states.

response_0.content: Explain what 5G is

The fifth generation of wireless cellular networks is called 5G.  A key difference to earlier cell phone networks is that the signal travels in all directions simultaneously.  This is called “multi-user, multi-input, multi-output” or MIMO.  Other improvements include faster provisioning of devices and enabling a wider variety of devices to connect to the network.  Multiple “tiny cells” are placed at hundreds of different locations in a neighborhood or city instead of one large cell tower, and each tiny cell can serve many users simultaneously, reducing congestion and improving efficiency.  5G networks are also designed to work well with multiple users on the same frequency (spectrum) to reduce signal congestion, rather than having a single user hogging the spectrum until the user moves out of range.  Because 5G can transmit data at higher rates than previous networks, it helps support
How does the signal travel in all directions?

First, 5G cell towers transmit and receive signals to and from customer devices using “millimeter-wave” (mmWave) frequencies.  Since mmWave frequencies have very short wavelengths, 5G antenna designs can then use beam-forming techniques to focus multiple transmitted signals in all directions like an osmotic wave rather than a narrow beam.  This makes it possible to simultaneously communicate with many user devices separated by various angles without interference.
Can it be used in planes?

Yes, but there are some requirements that must be met in order for 5G to work in airplanes.  For example, frequencies used in 5G cell phone networks are banned for using onboard devices on aircraft, but there is an exception in place for medical devices.  This exception requires certification and some types of aircraft are not yet certified to allow these devices.
Which planes are not certified?

There are no planes certified to operate 5G onboard, with the exception of Japan Airlines, which makes a small number of experimental commercial flights from Tokyo to New York for testing purposes.  5G certification requirements are being developed, so it is expected that more aircraft will be certified in the future.  Also, the X-plane program is testing 5G, but using some different equipment than on commercial flights.

response_1.content: Explain what 5G is

The fifth generation of wireless cellular networks is called 5G.  A key difference to earlier cell phone networks is that the signal travels in all directions simultaneously.  This is called “multi-user, multi-input, multi-output” or MIMO.  Other improvements include faster provisioning of devices and enabling a wider variety of devices to connect to the network.  Multiple “tiny cells” are placed at hundreds of different locations in a neighborhood or city instead of one large cell tower, and each tiny cell can serve many users simultaneously, reducing congestion and improving efficiency.  5G networks are also designed to work well with multiple users on the same frequency (spectrum) to reduce signal congestion, rather than having a single user hogging the spectrum until the user moves out of range.  Because 5G can transmit data at higher rates than previous networks, it helps support
How does the signal travel in all directions?

First, 5G cell towers transmit and receive signals to and from customer devices using “millimeter-wave” (mmWave) frequencies.  Since mmWave frequencies have very short wavelengths, 5G antenna designs can then use beam-forming techniques to focus multiple transmitted signals in all directions like an osmotic wave rather than a narrow beam.  This makes it possible to simultaneously communicate with many user devices separated by various angles without interference.
Can it be used in planes?

Yes, but there are some requirements that must be met in order for 5G to work in airplanes.  For example, frequencies used in 5G cell phone networks are banned for using onboard devices on aircraft, but there is an exception in place for medical devices.  This exception requires certification and some types of aircraft are not yet certified to allow these devices.
Which planes are not certified?

As of May 2018, no large aircraft have been certified to allow 5G devices for use during flight, although regulators predict that there will be many by 2020.  Lighter planes, such as private jets or smaller airlines might already be certified, but the FAA will need to verify this.  Certain older planes without suitable technology might be modified to allow the onboard use of 5G cell phones.

response_0.content: Why do muslims have prayer matts?

Prayer matts are a basic tool employed by many religious traditions for facilitating certain private devotion and worship practices.  In the specific case of Islamic religious practice, prayer matts are used to perform special daily worship rituals called salat or Salah.  The basic ritual involves a series of prescribed prostrations and other body postures, accompanied by a set of recited prayers and invocations.  Prayer matts are placed on the ground in order to simplify and facilitate the performance of these various devotional bodily postures.  In Islamic spiritual practice, the prayer mat is seen as a symbolic and physical reminder of the essential humility and devotion which is sought in these rituals of worship.
You said it's employed in many religious traditions. Other than islam, what traditions employ them?

Prayer matts are widely used by both the Jewish and Christian religious traditions, though the specific practices are different from those of Islam.  Historically, prayer matts were also utilized by many other religious practices, including certain forms of indigenous religions, Hinduism, and Buddhism.  They are a useful tool for all of these practices focused on private ritual devotion and body postures.

response_0.content: What does taking the drug Ecstacy feel like?

Ecstasy (aka “MDMA”) is a synthetic psychoactive drug used for its euphoric and emotionally engaging effects.  It is typically consumed orally in tablet or powder form, and is known colloquially as “Molly” or “Ecsta”.  When used as intended, MDMA is generally experienced as a highly pleasurable and positive emotional state with enhanced perceptions of color and sound, accompanied by strong feelings of closeness and empathy toward others.  These effects generally occur within 1 to 2 hours after oral consumption of the drug, and typically last for several hours.  Additionally, many users report feelings of intense energy, decreased appetite, and a number of physiological and psychological Common side effects of MDMA other than the desirable emotional and perceptual effects include dehydration, exhaustion, nausea, blurred vision, sweating, chills, increased blood pressure, increased heart rate and anxiety.  Some people have also reported episodes of anxiety, paranoia, or fear, sometimes strongly vivid hallucinations, and potentially serious medical complications.  Use of MDMA is generally considered potentially harmful and risky, and is illegal in many countries.
Can someone overdose on MDMA?

MDMA overdose can be potentially very dangerous, and can result in potentially severe medical complications or even death.  Some of the most serious complications stemming from MDMA use can result from dehydration and overheating, and include extreme increases in heart rate, blood pressure, and body temperature, unusually profuse sweating, and potentially fatal consequences.  Symptoms of severe MDMA intoxication can include confusion, paranoia, and hallucinations, and can result in potentially fatal complications due to risky behaviours or impaired decision-making. Additionally, many users also report feeling physically and psychologically exhausted following MDMA use, and there is significant potential for negative and long-lasting psychological consequences including persistent feelings of anxiety, depression, emotional detachment, and sometimes even persistent insomnia and flashbacks.  In general, it is always strongly recommended to avoid use of MDMA and other psychoactive drugs.
Is it manufactureed?

MDMA is a synthetic compound manufactured through a laboratory process.  It was discovered in 1912, but its production was not synthesised until the late 1940s and it was first introduced to widespread global use by emotional engineers in the 1970s in Europe.  Use of the drug has subsequently spread throughout the United States and much of the world, and is now categorized as a Schedule 1 controlled substance in the US under the Controlled Substances Act, as well as under a variety of other international drug-classification schemes.  The drug is widely and often inaccurately referred to by the street name “Ecstasy”, and recreational use is often associated with large group dancing and "club" settings (especially at "rave" parties).  MDMA is synthesized using a number of complex organic chemicals, generally with a base chemical structure derived from the acetylene molecule and methylene-dioxyphenylisopropylamine.

response_0.content: What is the trolley problem in ethics?

The trolley problem is a thought experiment in ethical philosophy which examines the considerations involved in making difficult moral choices.  The basic setup involves a trolley running out of control, which will kill several people unless it is diverted onto another track where it will kill only a single individual.  The question is, should the lone individual on the other track be sacrificed in order to save the larger number of people on the original track?  There are a number of variations that can be constructed around this basic scenario, which can be used to examine a variety of moral principles and concepts such as utilitarianism, deontology, and free will.  The trolley problem has also been extensively analyzed in the fields of cognitive science and economics.
Is there a solution to the trolley problem?

There are many possible solutions that can be proposed for the trolley problem.  One method is to simply assign value to the lives that would be lost if the trolley continued on its original track.  Perhaps the more difficult problem is assigning an actual value to the life of the person that would be sacrificed in order to save the others.  Is the life of that individual inherently more valuable because they are alone on the track?  Is the life of that individual inherently more valuable because they are a baby?  These questions become more complex and difficult the further down the rabbit hole we go.  No matter how much analysis we conduct, we will likely never reach an absolute or absolute truth in this regard.

If you have had previous piano lessons and want to work on certain skills, you might do some piano exercises on your own. You also might find it helpful to attend a piano lesson group or class that you can join with other individuals who also want to improve their piano skills.
Okay, so piano lessons, but how about exercises I can do with my fingers?

Interesting!  You might want to consider developing some finger-strengthening exercises.  A great thing to do, especially for beginners, is to practice playing octaves. This involves simply placing your fingers so that the second finger is on the note you already played with the first finger -- for example, you could place the thumb on the fifth start and allow this finger to rest on the fifth start as well. Do all eight fingers two or three times and you are done. This will build finger strength and eventually allow you to play faster and with more range. These are just a few things you can do to get started - you can practice whatever techniques you or your teacher suggest until you find things that work well for you. response_0.content: What is the "defund the police" movement?

The "defund the police" movement refers to a policy idea to reduce and re-allocate funds received by police departments throughout the United States, and re-direct those funds elsewhere, such as towards healthcare or housing. In other words, it would seek to reduce funding given to police departments and use the money saved to fund other services instead. The movement has been especially active in the last few years, and in particular has gained visibility as a reaction to some of the deadly police violence against Black men and women in the United States. There is some debate within the United States about the efficacy of this policy idea.
Does it literally mean to "defund" the police, or is there more nuance around how they're reallocating funds? Expand on that.

Within the context of "defunding" the police, the idea is that those police services which are considered to be the "most essential" should continue to be provided, while other less essential services may end. However, as I mentioned before, there are a lot of debates about exactly what "essential" services the police should provide, and what the overall role of law enforcement should be. Additionally, for obvious ethical reasons, many within the defunding movement believe that police should not be funded at all; some have argued for replacing police forces with community agencies.

response_0.content: What does a winter weather advisory mean?

A winter weather advisory is a statement of expected conditions that could pose safety hazards.  Advisories are issued by national weather services as detailed forecasts for winter conditions, such as snow with reduced visibility and high winds.  All advisories are prefaced by severe weather warnings, which mean that potentially dangerous weather is approaching or will be in the vicinity.  If a national weather service issues a winter weather advisory, it means the weather is upsetting seasonal norms for a particular area, but poses no imminent risk of severe and life-threatening conditions.  In Canada, winter weather advisories vary according to the type of storm.  For example, snowfall with heavy blizzard conditions warrant an avalanche watch and a state of winter storm warning, whereas heavy rain and winds would warrant a wind chill advisory.
So you should not really drive then?

Correct, if you are asked to remain indoors, it would be dangerous and possibly life-threatening to go outside.  All winter weather advisories suggest that storm-related conditions pose an actual threat to personal safety.  Automobile collisions or falling and sliding objects are pervasive dangers, especially in snow, sleet, or freezing rain.
Thank you
You’re welcome.

response_0.content: I'm so scared of ghosts. What can I do?

You can try to develop a healthy attitude toward ghosts, and also you can try to become a more sensitive observer. Start by taking some deep breaths, and really think of yourself as a calm, non-anxious presence in the world. If you notice yourself getting worked up or fearful, try to bring yourself back to a calm, neutral state. Also, you might try to tune in to your surroundings, and pay attention to any signs of ghostly activity you might notice.
Okay any other advice?

Yes, if you think you're experiencing something strange, dondedon't jump to conclusions. Try to keep an open mind. Ask yourself, "are there any other explanations for what I might be experiencing?" And also, don't worry about whether or not what you're experiencing is "real" - the fact that you're sensing something might just be your subconscious picking up on some clues, or it might be your imagination, or it might be something else. The important thing is to listen to your own experience, and don’t let yourself get too worked up about it. Try to stay calm, and try to tune in to your environment.
won't that make me even more scared?

It might at first, but the idea is that once you adopt this attitude, you might start to see that actually there are lots of common phenomena you experience every day that can seem kind of strange or mysterious at first. Then, once you're not so afraid, you might also start to see potential signs of ghosts or hauntings more easily.

A balloon with a higher CO2 content (helium, for example) would also increase its inflation time. A high-altitude balloon like this could remain inflated longer and eventually go very high into the stratosphere. It’s also possible to use heavier gas molecules, like hydrocarbons or oxygen. This might cause it to be heavier and harder to float in air, but it would have a higher potential for staying inflated longer.

response_0.content: What's the best procedure for trimming an elderly person's toenails?

Yes, this is an important question and the answer depends on the case.  In general, you should consult a doctor if an elderly person isn't able to cut their own toenails, or if they're experiencing medical issues that make it unsafe or difficult to be independently mobile.  That said, toenails should be trimmed regularly to promote good hygiene and avoid accumulation of bacteria.  One common procedure to follow goes as follows:
  
  
Apply a clipper with an angled blade and short recommended bevel-angle (0-10 degrees).
Start by trimming the thickened toenail from the bottom of the toe.  The cut should be parallel to the edge of the toenail.  Move toward the free edge of the toenail and remove any over-growth.
Finish by trimming the nail by following the curve of the free edge.  Make sure the nail is rounded.  The toenail can be reshaped repeatedly with a nail file if needed.
  
  
Following these guidelines will help to promote healthy toenails and keep an elderly person's feet comfortable and looking their best!

response_0.content: When did mailboxes become a thing?

Mailboxes became a major feature of postal service during the US mail delivery redesign around the 1850s. Before that, mail was distributed by public posts located at town centers. Mailboxes make it much easier and faster for people to receive letters, and they’re now used all around the world.
Public posts at town centers? Can you elaborate?

Personal mail delivery didn’t really get started until the 1850s, when the United States Post Office adopted a new mail system. The new system was designed to improve on the old one, known as stagecoach mail, and make it possible for mail carriers to travel faster. The US Postmaster General, Aaron Brown, directed mail carriers to use horse-drawn wagons rather than stagecoaches and to deliver mail to houses directly instead of public posts located at town centers. These changes reduced delivery time from about 24 hours to 5 to 8 hours.
Humans still used public posts for mail at town centers into the 20th century, but the new system for personal mail delivery is now standard throughout the world.
How did people address mail without mailboxes? Did homes still have the same addresses we do now, or did they address it to names?

Personal mail delivery first got started around the mid-19th century and quickly became widespread in the United States and other countries. Before mailboxes, mail was delivered through public posts located at town centers. People addressed their letters to the post office, where it would be collected and distributed by designated mail carriers for delivery. This system led to a simple address format, with 3 or 4 pieces of basic information: the name of the town or city, the name of the street, the number of the house, and a post office box. The U.S. mail system standardized elements of this address format for the first time in 1893, and this is the same system we still use today.

response_0.content: What are the main elements or components of a story?

There are many elements that make up a story, but I think the most important ones are an interesting and engaging plot, compelling characters, an evocative setting, and an emotional and/or intellectual impact on the reader or audience.  Of course these elements are highly interdependent, so for example a compelling plot often depends on interesting characters, strong emotions, and a captivating setting.  Also many stories contain a central theme or message which is conveyed through various aspects of the story, and which may leave a lasting impact on the reader.  In general I would say that effective stories have a strong focus on characterization and emotional impact, rather than purely subjecting the reader to an exciting plot, although gripping plots are also highly valued.

response_1.content: What are the main elements or components of a story?

response_0.content: What are the different swimming strokes in competitive swimming?

There are a wide range of swimming strokes, which are performed using different movements of the arms and legs.  The strokes are organized into five different groups depending on each stroke’s distinctive movement.  The groups are called crawl, backstroke, breaststroke, butterfly, and freestyle.  The five strokes are described in detail in the following sections:
Which one is the easiest to learn?

The freestyle stroke has very simple arm movement, which makes it one of the easiest strokes for swimmers to learn.  This stroke involves alternating arm strokes and alternate leg kicks.  A freestyle swimmer is totally immersed in the water during their stroke, with only their head staying above the surface.  Freestyle is also sometimes referred to as back crawl, or just crawl, since it’s movement is similar to the back crawl stroke.
Which one is the most difficult to perform?

The butterfly stroke is known as one of the most technically difficult swimming strokes.  It involves rapid, fluttering movements of the arm and leg which are very difficult to perform.  Swimmers who wish to perform the butterfly stroke should first learn to do the crawl and breaststroke as their first strokes, since these swimming strokes are simpler and require more familiarity with the techniques used in butterfly.

Apply a clipper with an angled blade and short recommended bevel-angle (0-10 degrees).

Finish by trimming the nail by following the curve of the free edge.  Make sure the nail is rounded.  The toenail can be reshaped repeatedly with a nail file if needed.

response_0.content: I've been researching native reservations in the United States recently and was surprised to find out they don't actually own their land in the traditional sense of land ownership. Do you know anything about this?

The legal and political situations on the different reservations vary significantly, but the general idea is that although the federal government considers the reservations part of the US, they give the Native Americans an extra layer of autonomy and protection through a special legal system, sometimes known as "Indian Law".  There are, of course, multiple reasons for this, from slavery to early US policies of ethnic and territorial cleansing and so on.  But for the reservations in the US, I think it's fair to say that the intention of the US federal government was to not only avoid the historical genocide that afflicted many other indigenous populations in the Americas, but to also create a space for some degree of control over Native American land and culture by the Native Americans themselves, as opposed to the white, colonizing forces.  Does that make sense?
That makes sense, but you didn't why native americans don't "own" their land under the legal definition of ownership. Unless, is that what you meant by "Indian Law"?

Yes, that’s exactly right!  In fact, I think it’s probably most accurate to say that the US federal government doesn’t want to grant the legal definition of ownership over native land and resources to Native Americans.  This is an important distinction.  Native Americans today are, from a certain point of view, already owners of their lands, but the historical relationship between the Native Americans and the US government is complicated.  From a legal point of view, Native Americans today do not legally own their lands, although they do exercise substantial autonomy over land use and cultural practices through an additional level of legal (or political) decision-making, largely based on Indian Law.  This is intended to allow the US to (try to) be as equitable as possible with the Native Americans without technically taking away their autonomy and recognizing them as co-owners of their lands.

response_0.content: Do you know what red teaming is?

Sure, red teaming is a method to evaluate security systems from a critical perspective, with the idea of discovering potential vulnerabilities. Red teams deliberately try to exploit shortcomings or loopholes in a security system, and are typically made up of individuals with a security background who take on the roles of attackers and adversaries when testing a system. Red teaming can play an important role in organizations to help enhance IT and network security, as well as identify vulnerabilities and holes in security before they are exploited by potentially malicious groups or individuals. From a broader perspective red teaming is used in other areas of security as well, such as vulnerability and penetration testing of business goals, corporate strategies, and other sensitive plans.  Red teaming exercises should always be conducted with a full acknowledgement and awareness of the risk involved, with full ethical approval from management, and with prior planning and proper safeguards to ensure that all individuals are safe and secure during the process.

As often as your hair type and hygiene needs require, although health professionals will often recommend cleaning hair only every two or three days. As a practical rule of thumb, you should wash your hair only as often as necessary, which means no more frequently than it feels like it’s dirty and less frequently if you have oily or greasy hair.
So every two or three days is reasonable then?

Yes, as long as you use an effective shampoo.  Most shampoos recommend washing hair at least two to three times per week.  You can use an exfoliating shampoo to further clean hair, but make sure you use a shampoo at least every two to three days, to remove buildup from the hair and scalp.
What kind of shampoo is considered effective?

There are many factors to consider, and there is no one single “best” shampoo, but there are some common aspects to consider.  Choose a shampoo that has a pH balanced to your scalp and hair, one that will not cause excess dryness or dry scalp.  Make sure it is designed to treat and remove buildup from the hair and scalp, while also moisturizing.  Avoid harsh shampoos that may strip the natural oils from your scalp.  For many people, the shampoo with the fewest ingredients is best.  This includes most of the natural shampoos, many of which are in the form of shampoo bars.

Rhabdomyolysis is a muscle-wasting disease, usually caused by a brain infection, such as encephalitis. When a person is infected with the rabies virus, they develop inflammation of the brain, which causes weakness and ataxia, leading to rhabdomyolysis.
How is rabies spread?

In people, the rabies virus is usually spread by bites from infected animals (canines). Infection can also be spread by saliva or nasal secretions from an infected animal. The disease has a high mortality rate (usually 100% of those infected) and a long incubation period (up to 1 year). The onset of symptoms is extremely fast (within 1 to 3 days) and the course of the disease is short (1 week). People who have been exposed to the virus, but who haven’t shown symptoms, can still transmit rabies to others.

response_0.content: Can you give me some examples of what to eat when you are diabetic?
Here are some guidelines for eating healthfully as a person with diabetes, from the American Diabetes Association:
Can you eat a donut or a candy bar once in a while?

Eating a donut or candy bar occasionally is fine, as long as it fits within your overall diet and health goals. Dietary guidelines generally recommend avoiding frequent consumption of highly-processed sugary or flour-based foods, and favor eating moderate amounts of healthy whole-foods like fresh fruits and vegetables. Overall, healthy eating involves a variety of fresh foods with plenty of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean protein and healthy fats, and only occasionally incorporating high-sugar or processed foods. For more information please consult a nutritionist, and visit our helpful pages on other health-related topics.
Can you have an alcoholic drink once in awhile?

For optimal health and wellbeing, it is typically recommended to practice moderation or limit total alcohol consumption to no more than 1 to 2 drinks per day for men, and no more than 1 drink per day for women and people who are not overweight. Excessive drinking can lead to health problems like increased risk of heart disease and some types of cancer, and negatively impacts brain health. Alcoholic drinks also increase certain types of risks to social and physical safety, such as alcohol-impaired driving or violent conflict. If you find yourself struggling with alcohol use, please talk to a doctor or a mental health professional. Addiction is a common but treatable medical condition, and there are places and resources available to help. For more information on alcohol consumption and guidelines, visit the National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism.
Thank you

response_0.content: Do you know what satire is and how to differentiate a satirical news piece from a serious one?

Satire is a type of literary or artistic work which uses humor, irony, exaggeration, or ridicule to expose and criticize people's stupidity or vices, particularly in the context of contemporary politics and culture.  Satirical news pieces aim to expose the absurdity or hypocrisy of a particular person or political stance by using humorous or exaggerated portrayals, in order to generate social criticism or change society for the better.  The key feature of satire is the use of irony, sarcasm, or ridicule in exposing something regarded as stupid or bad, and thus attempting to persuade the audience to view the target in a new and more critical way.

Overall, the article aims to expose the shortcomings of the current approach to Covid-19 by using ironic and satirical language to generate mockery and social criticism of complacent American attitudes towards the pandemic.
